subject: Re: warp flipping part of the image to the other end
body:
Thanks for the replies! 

alist...@gmail.com schrieb am Freitag, 9. Juli 2021 um 15:57:49 UTC+2:

> Hi Matthias,
>
> The problem is related to the mrc header - you can correct the header by 
> running the file through an image processing tool which generates correct 
> headers. There’s some discussion about this here 
> https://groups.google.com/g/warp-em/c/YDTyETcqUF0/m/VVscoUa9AwAJ
>
> Given that you likely already have IMOD installed on the local machine, I 
> would try passing the files through newstack
>
> newstack input.mrc output.mrc
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> On 9 Jul 2021, at 14:39, Matthias Vorländer <mvorl...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> Hi there, 
>
> I am processing falcon 4  SPA data in MRC format (gain normalized) in warp 
> , and warp is producing a strange artefact where part of the image is 
> flipped on the other side (see screenshot). Any idea what causes this?
>
> Thanks a lot in advance,
> Best,
> Matthias
>
>
> --
